frozen_components: Base LLM parameters (frozen during Reasoning Projector SFT initialization)
architecture.summary: LiteReason augments a base LLM (Qwen 2.5 7B-Instruct) with a lightweight Reasoning Projector module—a stack of MLP layers that predicts continuous token embeddings. During generation, the model operates in two modes: discrete mode (standard token sampling via LM head) and latent reasoning mode (Reasoning Projector produces continuous embeddings). The model learns to emit special implicit-thought tags that trigger latent mode and specify how many latent reasoning steps to take. Information flows as follows: (1) standard hidden states → LM head → discrete tokens until an implicit-thought tag is encountered; (2) upon tag detection, hidden states → Reasoning Projector → continuous embeddings (fed back as next-token inputs) for a fixed number of steps; (3) then switches back to discrete mode. The Reasoning Projector is trained via supervised fine-tuning on rejection-sampled reasoning traces with randomly masked reasoning steps; the base LLM is then trained via GRPO reinforcement learning to decide when to activate latent reasoning and improve task performance. Periodic SFT updates re-sync the Reasoning Projector with evolving model reasoning.
trained_components: Base LLM parameters (trained via GRPO during RL phase)

biblio.title: Lightweight Latent Reasoning for Narrative Tasks

claims.statement: LiteReason reduces final reasoning length by 77–92% while approaching non-latent RL training performance
On Flawed Fictions, LiteReason achieves 87.42% accuracy, gaining 30.16 percentage points over the untrained baseline (57.26%) and approaching RL-Trained performance (88.71%)
On Flawed Fictions, LiteReason generates 34.01 average tokens, reducing output length by 91.50% compared to the untrained baseline (400.04 tokens)
On Next Chapter Prediction, LiteReason achieves 0.478 Contrastive Improvement, gaining 613.43% over the untrained baseline (0.067) and 71.8% of the RL-Trained gain (0.666)
On Next Chapter Prediction, LiteReason generates 193.11 average tokens, reducing output length by 77.50% compared to the untrained baseline (831.85 tokens)
During RL training, LiteReason requires 52.8% fewer discrete tokens on Flawed Fictions (29.0M vs. 61.4M) and 49.5% fewer on Next Chapter Prediction (54.9M vs. 108.8M) compared to standard RL-Trained

baseline.name: COCONUT, CoLaR, Mixture of Inputs, Soft Thinking

claims.dataset: Flawed Fictions and Next Chapter Prediction
claims.statement: LiteReason significantly outperforms training-free methods (Mixture of Inputs, Soft Thinking) and trained latent reasoning baselines (COCONUT, CoLaR) on both Flawed Fictions and Next Chapter Prediction tasks

LiteReason w/o SFT (without periodic Reasoning Projector updates during RL) produces more tokens on Next Chapter Prediction (622.23 vs. 193.11 for full LiteReason) despite achieving higher Contrastive Improvement (0.560 vs. 0.478), indicating a trade-off between performance and efficiency that periodic SFT helps resolve
